result.stdout:
JetBrains TeamCity C# 10.0 script runner 1.0.7 net8.0

Usage: dotnet csi [options] [--] [script] [script arguments]

Executes a script if specified, otherwise launches an interactive REPL (Read Eval Print Loop).

script
    The path to the script file to run.
    If no such file is found, the command will treat it as a directory
    and look for a single script file inside that directory.

script arguments
    Script arguments are accessible in a script via the global list Args[index] by an argument index.

--
    Indicates that the remaining arguments should not be treated as options.

--help
    Display this usage message (alternative forms: /? -h /h /help).

--version
    Display the version and exit (alternative form: /version).

--source <NuGet package source>
    NuGet package source (URL, UNC/folder path) to use (alternative forms: -s /source /s),
    for example: --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json

--property <key=value>[;<keyN=valueN>]
    Define a key-value pair(s) for the script properties (*1) accessible in scripts.
    Alternative forms:
        -property
        -p
        /property
        /p
    Specify each property separately, or use a semicolon or comma to separate multiple properties,
    as the following example shows: --property key1=val1;key2=val2

--property:<key=value>[;<keyN=valueN>]
    Define a key-value pair(s) in MSBuild style for the script properties (*1) accessible in scripts.
    Alternative forms:
        -property:<key=value>
        -p:<key=value>
        /property:<key=value>
        /p:<key=value>
    Specify each property separately, or use a semicolon or comma to separate multiple properties,
    as the following example shows: --property:key1=val1;key2=val2

@<file>
    Read response file for more options as the following example shows: @OptionsDir/MyOptions.rsp

Notes:
    - *1 script properties are accessible a script via the global dictionary Props["name"] by a property name
    - using HostApi; directive in a script allows you to use host API types
      without specifying the fully qualified namespace of these types
